item.title: Yara Boosts Profit as Iran War Sends Fertilizer Prices Soaring
item.description: Fertilizer giant Yara International ASA posted higher-than-expected first quarter earnings as the Iran war effectively halted transit through the Strait of Hormuz, hurting global trade of crop nutrients and pushing up prices.

item.title: Specialist Engineering Firm TSK Plans €150 Million IPO in Spain
item.description: TSK Electronica y Electricidad SA is planning an initial public offering in what would be the first sizeable main market listing in Spain this year.

item.title: India’s Shapoorji Gets Nod to Delay Bond Days Before Maturity
item.description: Indian infrastructure conglomerate Shapoorji Pallonji Group has secured creditor approval to delay payment on one of the nation’s biggest high-yield bonds, just days ahead of the April 30 maturity, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: China Imposes Export Curbs on EU Firms Over Taiwan Arms Sales
item.description: China added seven European entities to its export control list, citing national security interests and their involvement in arms sales to Taiwan.

item.title: China Scaled Back Fiscal Stimulus in First Month of War in Iran
item.description: China pulled back on fiscal spending in March as the economy rebounded at the start of the year despite disruptions caused by the war in Iran.

item.title: German Business Outlook at Worst Since 2023 as War Hits ‘Hard’
item.description: Germany’s business outlook deteriorated more than expected, adding to fears that higher energy costs stemming from the Iran conflict will derail the country’s economic revival.

item.title: One by One, Europe’s Safest Government Bonds Fall From Grace
item.description: The traditional hierarchy of debt in Europe is facing its latest shake-up as worsening public finances in Belgium threaten to turn some of the region’s once-safest bonds into a risky bet.

item.title: Malaysia’s Anwar Eyes Polls by October as Fuel Subsidy Cut Looms
item.description: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim is considering calling for a general election in the third quarter as his government weighs politically sensitive cuts to fuel subsidies amid the global energy crunch, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Europe’s LNG Imports Set for First Monthly Drop in Over a Year
item.description: Europe is facing its first monthly drop in seaborne supply of natural gas in over a year, with volumes dampened by terminal works and tighter global flows.

item.title: James Bosworth on the ‘Orange Wave’ Happening Across Latin America
item.description: From Delcy to Milei.

item.title: Schlegel Says Vigilant SNB Unrestricted on Rates, Interventions
item.description: The Swiss National Bank is closely monitoring fallout from the Iran war and can steer the franc’s exchange rate without limits if necessary, according to President Martin Schlegel.

item.title: Odd Lots: The “Orange Wave” Realigning Latin America (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: The “Orange Wave” Realigning Latin America (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: The “Orange Wave” Realigning Latin America (Podcast)】

item.title: China’s Eoptolink Is Said to Pick Banks for $3 Billion Hong Kong Listing
item.description: Eoptolink Technology Inc., a Chinese maker of optical transceivers, has picked banks for a listing in Hong Kong that could raise about $3 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: China’s Oil Stockpiles Remain Robust as Teapots Crank Up Output
item.description: China has kept its massive crude stockpiles intact, despite a collapse in Middle Eastern flows due to the Iran war, with the much-maligned private refiners playing a pivotal role.

item.title: Iran War to Keep Gas Market Tight for Two More Years, IEA Says
item.description: The global natural gas market is set to remain tight well beyond this year, as the ongoing conflict in the Middle East and damage to regional infrastructure continue to disrupt supply, according to the International Energy Agency.

item.title: European Stock Returns Are Now Losing Against the US This Year
item.description: European stocks have lost their outperformance edge over the US market as traders worry about the toll of persistently high oil prices on economic growth.

item.title: Nomura Posts Record Full-Year Profit on Japan Market Rebound
item.description: Nomura Holdings Inc. cemented a second straight year of record profit, fueled by Japan’s financial-market recovery, even after fourth-quarter results missed analysts’ expectations.

item.title: Bank of England Official Says Share Prices Don’t Reflect Risks
item.description: A senior Bank of England official warned that high asset prices didn’t reflect the level of risk in the markets, suggesting that global markets could fall.

item.title: Waterland Raises €4 Billion for Latest Private Equity Fund
item.description: European private equity firm Waterland has raised €4 billion ($4.7 billion) for its new flagship fund, signaling an uptick in demand for European mid-market funds.

item.title: Goldman Says Persian Gulf Oil Supply Is 57% Below Pre-War Levels
item.description: Oil output from Persian Gulf nations has been running 14.5 million barrels a day below pre-war levels this month, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc., which estimated any resumption would take months.

item.title: Norway Set to Become Latest Country to Ban Social Media for Under 16s
item.description: Norway plans to restrict access to social media for children under 16, joining a growing number of countries responding to concerns about the potential harm kids face online.

item.title: Lightelligence Is Said Set to Price HK IPO at Top of Range
item.description: Chinese optical-computing company Lightelligence is poised to price its oversubscribed Hong Kong initial public offering at the top of the marketed range, according to people familiar with the matter, signaling hot demand for components used in the artificial-intelligence buildout.

item.title: UK Retail Sales Rebound as Drivers Stock Up on Fuel
item.description: UK retail sales unexpectedly bounced back in March, with the country’s motorists rushing to forecourts to fill up on fuel as petrol prices surged in light of conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Funders Commit $1.3 Billion to Zambia Critical-Minerals Railway
item.description: The Africa Finance Corp. and the African Development Bank committed $500 million each to a railway that will connect Zambia’s copper mines to global markets through the Angolan port of Lobito.

item.title: Read Japan Finance Minister Katayama’s Full Remarks at Bloomberg New Voices
item.description: The following is a transcript of Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama’s remarks at the Bloomberg New Voices 2026 event in Tokyo on April 23 in conversation with anchor Shery Ahn.

item.title: Stock Futures Fall and Pound Steadies as Iran Talks Stall
Behind the 40,000% Stock Surge That’s Mystifying Traders in Turkey
item.description: At first, it was just a curiosity in Turkey’s financial circles: An obscure brokerage firm with a stock that went up and up.

item.title: India Cranks Up Output to Cope With Enduring Cooking Gas Crisis
item.description: India’s government is leaning on its refineries to survive an acute shortage of cooking gas as the war in Iran drags on, while also scouring the world for additional suppliers and nudging consumers toward alternatives.

item.title: Pistachio Price Shock Spurs South Africa Farmers to Boost Output
item.description: South African farming group Karoo Pistachios is ramping up output to capitalize on surging prices and compete with the world’s biggest producers.

item.title: KKR, Capital Group to Start Public-Private Credit Fund in Asia
item.description: KKR & Co. and Capital Group plan to launch a public-private credit fund in Asia this year, in the latest tie-up between private-markets specialists and traditional asset managers seeking to attract more retail money.
